children.name: info
help: Show OS & kernel details of the memory sample being analyzed.
children.terminal_help:
usage: vol.py windows.info.Info [-h]

Show OS & kernel details of the memory sample being analyzed.

options:
  -h, --help  show this help message and exit
children.comment: Note: In volatility 2 imageinfo try to find the best profile to use, in volatility 3 they are no profile so info is just giving info

children.name: crashinfo
help: Lists the information from a Windows crash dump.
children.terminal_help:
usage: vol.py windows.crashinfo.Crashinfo [-h]

Lists the information from a Windows crash dump.

options:
  -h, --help  show this help message and exit

children.name: statistics
help: Lists statistics about the memory space (about valid/invalid pages)
children.terminal_help:
usage: vol.py windows.statistics.Statistics [-h]

Lists statistics about the memory space.

options:
  -h, --help  show this help message and exit

children.name: bigpools
help: List big page pools.
children.terminal_help:
usage: vol.py windows.bigpools.BigPools [-h] [--tags TAGS] [--show-free]

List big page pools.

options:
  -h, --help   show this help message and exit
  --tags TAGS  Comma separated list of pool tags to filter pools returned
  --show-free  Show freed regions (otherwise only show allocations in use)

children.name: poolscanner
help: A generic pool scanner plugin.
This plugin is scanning the whole memory for any strucutres he knows
This scanner is more useful for creating new plugin.
children.terminal_help:
usage: vol.py windows.poolscanner.PoolScanner [-h]

A generic pool scanner plugin.

options:
  -h, --help  show this help message and exit
children.comment: Pool scanning is a technique used by volatility to find particula object inside the memory (he looks for known structures like EPROCESS, ETHREAD, etc.)

children.name: kpcrs
help: Print KPCR structure for each processor
KPCR is a structure containing per-CPU information.
There are as many KPCR in the system as there are CPUs
It stores architectural-specific information that the processor needs to function, such as:
- Interrupt Dispatcher Tables (IDT)
- Global Descriptor Tables (GDT)
- Task State Segments (TSS)
- The current IRQL (Interrupt Request Level)
children.terminal_help:
usage: vol.py windows.kpcrs.KPCRs [-h]

Print KPCR structure for each processor

options:
  -h, --help  show this help message and exit

children.name: debugregisters
help: x86 and x64 processors have specific hardware registers dedicated to debugging, this plugin read these registers to find the hardware breakpoints.
children.terminal_help:
usage: vol.py windows.debugregisters.DebugRegisters [-h]

options:
  -h, --help  show this help message and exit

children.name: windowstations
help: Scans for top level Windows Stations
children.terminal_help:
usage: vol.py windows.windowstations.WindowStations [-h]

Scans for top level Windows Stations

options:
  -h, --help  show this help message and exit

usage: vol.py windows.desktops.Desktops [-h]

Enumerates the Desktop instances of each Window Station

options:
  -h, --help  show this help message and exit

Enumerates the Windows of Desktop instances

options:

children.name: pslist
help: List the processes in memory at the time of the dump
This plugin find processes following the doubly-linked EPROCESS list
children.terminal_help:
usage: vol.py windows.pslist.PsList [-h] [--physical] [--pid [PID ...]]
                                    [--dump]

Lists the processes present in a particular windows memory image.

options:
  -h, --help       show this help message and exit
  --physical       Display physical offsets instead of virtual
  --pid [PID ...]  Process ID to include (all other processes are excluded)
  --dump           Extract listed processes

children.name: pstree
help: Plugin for listing processes in a tree based on their parent process ID.
children.terminal_help:
usage: vol.py windows.pstree.PsTree [-h] [--physical] [--pid [PID ...]]

Plugin for listing processes in a tree based on their parent process ID.

options:
  -h, --help       show this help message and exit
  --physical       Display physical offsets instead of virtual
  --pid [PID ...]  Process ID to include (with ancestors and descendants, all
                   other processes are excluded)
children.comment: Just like pslist, only the output appearance change

children.name: psscan
help: List the processes in memory at the time of the dump
psscan contrary to pslist, and like all the 'scan' plugin, is using pool scanning to find any EPROCESS structure
children.terminal_help:
usage: vol.py windows.psscan.PsScan [-h] [--pid [PID ...]] [--dump]
                                    [--physical]

Scans for processes present in a particular windows memory image.

options:
  -h, --help       show this help message and exit
  --pid [PID ...]  Process ID to include (all other processes are excluded)
  --dump           Extract listed processes
  --physical       Display physical offset instead of virtual

children.name: psxview
help: Lists all processes found via four of the methods described in "The Art of Memory Forensics"
mostly used to find unlinked and/or hidden process
children.terminal_help:
usage: vol.py windows.malware.psxview.PsXView [-h] [--physical-offsets]

Lists all processes found via four of the methods described in "The Art of Memory Forensics"
which may help identify processes that are trying to hide
themselves.

options:
  -h, --help          show this help message and exit
  --physical-offsets  List processes with physical offsets instead of virtual
                      offsets.

We recommend using -r pretty if you are looking at this plugin's output in a
terminal.
children.comment: Grep and 'False' to spot processes present via one method but not the other

children.name: sessions
help: lists Processes with Session information extracted from Environmental
children.terminal_help:
usage: vol.py windows.sessions.Sessions [-h] [--pid [PID ...]]

lists Processes with Session information extracted from Environmental
Variables

options:
  -h, --help       show this help message and exit
  --pid [PID ...]  Process IDs to include (all other processes are excluded)

children.name: verinfo
help: Lists version information from PE files.
children.terminal_help:
usage: vol.py windows.verinfo.VerInfo [-h] [--extensive]

Lists version information from PE files.

options:
  -h, --help   show this help message and exit
  --extensive  Search physical layer for version information

children.name: cmdline
help: Lists process command line arguments.
children.terminal_help:
usage: vol.py windows.cmdline.CmdLine [-h] [--pid [PID ...]]

Lists process command line arguments.

options:
  -h, --help       show this help message and exit
  --pid [PID ...]  Process IDs to include (all other processes are excluded)

children.name: cmdscan
help: Looks for Windows Command History lists.
Scan every conhost.exe process to spot history from them
children.terminal_help:
usage: vol.py windows.cmdscan.CmdScan [-h] [--no-registry]
                                      [--max-history [MAX_HISTORY ...]]

Looks for Windows Command History lists

options:
  -h, --help            show this help message and exit
  --no-registry         Don't search the registry for possible values of
                        CommandHistorySize
  --max-history [MAX_HISTORY ...]
                        CommandHistorySize values to search for.
children.comment: Since Windows 7, the command line history is not managed by csrss like in the past but each conhost.exe processes have it's own command history structure

children.name: consoles
help: Looks for Windows console buffers
cmdscan is only printing the command he find, consoles will also look for the outputs
children.terminal_help:
usage: vol.py windows.consoles.Consoles [-h] [--no-registry]
                                        [--max-history [MAX_HISTORY ...]]
                                        [--max-buffers [MAX_BUFFERS ...]]

Looks for Windows console buffers

options:
  -h, --help            show this help message and exit
  --no-registry         Don't search the registry for possible values of
                        CommandHistorySize and HistoryBufferMax
  --max-history [MAX_HISTORY ...]
                        CommandHistorySize values to search for.
  --max-buffers [MAX_BUFFERS ...]
                        HistoryBufferMax values to search for.

children.name: dlllist
help: [--ignore-case] [--dump]
children.terminal_help:
usage: vol.py windows.dlllist.DllList [-h] [--pid [PID ...]] [--offset OFFSET]
                                      [--base BASE] [--name NAME]
                                      [--ignore-case] [--dump]

Lists the loaded DLLs in a particular windows memory image.

options:
  -h, --help       show this help message and exit
  --pid [PID ...]  Process IDs to include (all other processes are excluded)
  --offset OFFSET  Process offset in the physical address space
  --base BASE      Specify a base virtual address in process memory
  --name NAME      Specify a regular expression to match dll name(s)
  --ignore-case    Specify case insensitivity for the regular expression name
                   matching
  --dump           Extract listed DLLs

                                      [--maxsize MAXSIZE]

options:

  --address ADDRESS  Process virtual memory address to include (all other
                     address ranges are excluded).
  --pid [PID ...]    Filter on specific process IDs

                     sections will be ignored)

help: [--maxsize MAXSIZE]

usage: vol.py windows.vadregexscan.VadRegExScan [-h] [--pid [PID ...]]
                                                --pattern PATTERN
                                                [--maxsize MAXSIZE]

Scans all virtual memory areas for tasks using RegEx.

options:
  -h, --help         show this help message and exit
  --pid [PID ...]    Filter on specific process IDs
  --pattern PATTERN  RegEx pattern
  --maxsize MAXSIZE  Maximum size in bytes for displayed context

children.name: vadyarascan
help: [--yara-file YARA_FILE]
children.terminal_help:
usage: vol.py windows.vadyarascan.VadYaraScan [-h] [--insensitive] [--wide]
                                              [--yara-string YARA_STRING]
                                              [--yara-file YARA_FILE]
                                              [--yara-compiled-file YARA_COMPILED_FILE]
                                              [--max-size MAX_SIZE]
                                              [--pid [PID ...]]

Scans all the Virtual Address Descriptor memory maps using yara.

options:
  -h, --help            show this help message and exit
  --insensitive         Makes the search case insensitive
  --wide                Match wide (unicode) strings
  --yara-string YARA_STRING
                        Yara rules (as a string)
  --yara-file YARA_FILE
                        Yara rules (as a file)
  --yara-compiled-file YARA_COMPILED_FILE
                        Yara compiled rules (as a file)
  --max-size MAX_SIZE   Set the maximum size (default is 1GB)
  --pid [PID ...]       Process IDs to include (all other processes are
                        excluded)

children.name: netstat

options:

  --include-corrupt  Radically eases result validation. This will show
                     partially overwritten data. WARNING: the results are
                     likely to include garbage and/or corrupt data. Be
                     cautious!

children.name: psxview
help: Lists all processes found via four of the methods described in "The Art of
children.terminal_help:
usage: vol.py windows.malware.psxview.PsXView [-h] [--physical-offsets]

Memory Forensics" which may help identify processes that are trying to hide
themselves.

options:

  --physical-offsets  List processes with physical offsets instead of virtual
                      offsets.

We recommend using -r pretty if you are looking at this plugin's output in a
terminal.

  --insensitive         Makes the search case insensitive
  --wide                Match wide (unicode) strings

  --max-size MAX_SIZE   Set the maximum size (default is 1GB)

children.name: etwpatch
help: Identifies ETW (Event Tracing for Windows) patching techniques used by malware
children.terminal_help:
usage: vol.py windows.etwpatch.EtwPatch [-h] [--pid [PID ...]]

Identifies ETW (Event Tracing for Windows) patching techniques used by malware
to evade detection.

options:
  -h, --help       show this help message and exit
  --pid [PID ...]  Filter on specific process IDs

This plugin examines the first opcode of key ETW functions in ntdll.dll and
advapi32.dll to detect common ETW bypass techniques such as return pointer
manipulation (RET) or function redirection (JMP). Attackers often patch these
functions to prevent security tools from receiving telemetry about process
execution, API calls, and other system events.

children.name: pe_symbols
help: [--symbols [SYMBOLS ...]]
children.terminal_help:
usage: vol.py windows.pe_symbols.PESymbols [-h] --source {kernel,processes}
                                           --module MODULE
                                           [--symbols [SYMBOLS ...]]
                                           [--addresses [ADDRESSES ...]]

Prints symbols in PE files in process and kernel memory

options:
  -h, --help            show this help message and exit
  --source {kernel,processes}
                        Where to resolve symbols.
  --module MODULE       Module in which to resolve symbols. Use "ntoskrnl.exe"
                        to resolve in the base kernel executable.
  --symbols [SYMBOLS ...]
                        Symbol name to resolve
  --addresses [ADDRESSES ...]
                        Address of symbol to resolve
